facts.claim: Zakynthos, also known as Zante, is a Greek island in the Ionian Sea.

Zakynthos,[a] also known as Zante,[b] is a Greek island in the Ionian Sea. It is the third largest of the Ionian Islands, with an area of 405.55 km2 (156.6 sq mi),[5] and a coastline 123 km (76 mi) in length. In Greek mythology, the island was said to be named after Zacynthus, the son of the legendary Arcadian chief Dardanus.
